rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, underlined. No amounts present.
Clear centered section title beneath the year. No amounts in the title.
Legible label at upper right of the first account block. No monetary figures on the label itself.

Name 'Hays' is clear. The small three-column amount to the right is faint; the leftmost of the three (pounds) looks like '2', shillings '3' and pence '3' by alignment with the ruling lines, but ink is light so there is moderate uncertainty.
Name legible. Amount shows a clear '18' in the pounds column and '4' in the shillings column; pence column appears blank (interpreted as 0d). Alignment with vertical column lines is consistent.
Name clear. Amount columns read as pounds '1', shillings '0', pence '3' with good alignment. Pence digit is distinct.
Name 'Symm' clear. Right-hand three-column numbers read as '1' (pounds), '3' (shillings), '10' (pence). Digits are legible and pass currency validation.
Name legible. Numeric columns aligned: '1' (pounds), '14' (shillings), '7' (pence) — all within valid ranges and clearly placed between ruling lines.

Name is clear. Pounds and shillings read as '19' and '19' respectively; pence appears to be '9'. The handwriting of the tens digits is slightly flourishy which introduces modest uncertainty but alignment supports this reading.
Name clearly 'Jones'. Amount columns read '4' (pounds), '15' (shillings), '6' (pence); digits are distinct and aligned.

Description text is slightly cramped but legible as 'Neighbour Map of Brasen Estate'. Amount reads '14' in pounds and '1' in shillings; pence column looks blank (taken as 0). Some letters in the description are faint.

Description 'Consols purchase of Masters Comp.' is readable though slightly abbreviated. Numeric alignment suggests '13' pounds, '1' shilling and possibly '6' pence — pence digit is somewhat faint, so moderate ambiguity remains.

Text explicitly gives '£1.11' and stamps '5' (interpreted as 5d), the combined figure appears written as 1 pound 11s 10d by the small three-column numbers. The pence '10' is slightly compressed but readable.
Clearly written deduction 'Gile & Ward 5.2'. Interpreted as 5s 2d; no pounds shown. Ink and alignment are clear.
Small subtotal line clearly showing '1.11.10' (1 pound, 11 shillings, 10 pence). Positioned as a calculation result beneath the small journey/stamp/deduction items.

Description 'Sheppard & Paper Rooms' is legible though the handwriting is slightly flourished. The amount to the right aligns with '1 11 10' but the line is close to the previous subtotal — moderate ambiguity whether this is a separate item or part of the prior small group; treated as an entry because it is on its own text line.

Text 'Undercharge Manton Channel' is readable. The numeric to the right reads '2 2' which I interpret as 2s 2d (no pounds column figure). Alignment with the shillings/pence lines supports this reading.

This row in the image shows a multi-part stacked number (appears as '25 48 15 7' in the scan). The spacing and extra digits make direct mapping to the three columns unclear. I have chosen to interpret the visual group conservatively as pounds '25', shillings '4', pence '8' to keep shillings <20 and pence <12. Serious ambiguity exists because the original scribble seems to contain extra grouped figures; confidence is low. (If there is a secondary internal column, it is not unambiguously separable here.)
Clear centered Latin heading for the totals section. No amounts on the heading itself.
Clear centered date below the Summa heading.
The 'Accepta.' line on the right side clearly shows pounds '4122', shillings '16', pence '11' in the rightmost set of three ruling lines. The four-digit pounds figure is legible and aligned.

Text 'Soluta - Ordinaria' is clear. The numeric cluster reads as '12 29 13 1' in the scan; interpreted as a four-digit pounds '1229', shillings '13', pence '1'. The spacing between the '12' and '29' in the scan makes this slightly ambiguous, but alignment with the rightmost ruling lines supports 1229 as pounds.
The 'Varia' line on the right side shows a clear multi-thousand pounds figure '2548', with shillings '15' and pence '7'. Digits are well aligned in the right-hand columns.
Line reads 'Deficit Nov.1864' and the amounts align as pounds '1655', shillings '7', pence '3'. The numerals are distinct and consistent with the right-hand columns.
Prominent total line on the right with pounds '5433', shillings '15', pence '11'. Clear heavy underline and well-aligned digits make this very certain.
Centered label 'Solutis' (looks like a small ledger summary) below the big totals. No amount printed on the label line.
The word 'Solutis' repeated with the same total amount written to the right: pounds '5433', shillings '15', pence '11'. Clear repetition confirms earlier total.
Line 'Receptis' shows amounts '4122' pounds, '16' shillings, '11' pence matching prior 'Accepta' figure; digits are clear and aligned.

The final deficit figure appears as a three- or four-part group in the scan. I read this as pounds '1310', shillings '19', pence '0'. The shillings '19' is legible; the pounds digits are slightly connected in the handwriting producing moderate ambiguity between '1310' and possible close variants (e.g. '1370'); chosen value fits column alignment and keeps shillings/pence valid.
Signature block heading near bottom of page. No monetary amounts.

rows.description: Signatures (several names; signatures not transcribed line-by-line)
rows.notes: Several handwritten signatures follow (e.g. Whitford, Rector, others). These are not monetary rows. Individual names are in cursive and partly overlapping; treated as non-amount entries.
